title: Implement Rust Markdown reader MVP
description: Scaffold and implement the Rust desktop application with file argument loading, editable plaintext mode, formatted Markdown mode, minimal toggle navigation, save behavior, and section-aware scroll preservation.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo project exists; app opens provided Markdown path; content can be edited in plaintext; Ctrl+S or close persists changes; formatted view updates after edits; section-aware toggle behavior keeps the corresponding document area visible.
notes:
Implementation delegated to a worker after audit prompt-run record creation. Chosen starting approach: Wry/WebView2 shell, pulldown-cmark Markdown rendering, ammonia sanitization, DOM-based textarea/render scroll preservation by Markdown section.
Implementation worker outcome: added initial Rust/Wry Markdown reader MVP in Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, src/main.rs, and README.md. Behavior implemented: exactly-one path argument validation, large resizable Wry/WebView2 window, sparse nav toggle, rendered-default Markdown view, plaintext editing, Ctrl+S save, close autosave, Rust pulldown-cmark rendering with ammonia sanitization, and heading-index based scroll restoration between textarea and rendered view. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo build, argument smoke checks for zero/two/missing file args, and bounded GUI startup smoke with a temp Markdown file. Status: complete from worker perspective; ready for orchestrator review.
User clarified the app should include a normal Windows title bar for resize/maximize/close. Existing Wry default likely provided decorations, but implementation now explicitly sets with_decorations(true) on the WindowBuilder.
Orchestrator review accepted the implementation with one direct clarification patch: WindowBuilder now explicitly uses with_decorations(true), satisfying the normal Windows title bar requirement for resize/maximize/close. cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo build, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, CLI error-path smoke checks, and bounded GUI startup smoke all passed.
close_reason: Rust Markdown reader/editor MVP implemented and orchestrator-reviewed. Includes explicit normal title bar decorations, file argument loading, sparse toggle nav, plaintext editing, rendered Markdown view, save behavior, safe rendering, and heading-based location preservation.

title: Validate Markdown reader build and behavior
description: Run formatting, tests where available, cargo check/build, and a lightweight smoke validation for the Markdown reader. Record any limitations or follow-up work.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt/check/build outcomes recorded; smoke validation for argument handling and source structure recorded; unresolved manual UI risks captured in Beads if any.
notes:
Validation started by orchestrator after implementation review.
Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check; cargo test (2 tests passed); cargo build; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; CLI smoke for zero args, two args, and missing file all returned exit 1 with expected usage/read errors; bounded GUI startup smoke with temp Markdown file stayed alive for 5 seconds and was terminated.
close_reason: Validation suite passed: fmt, check, tests, build, clippy, argument smoke, and bounded GUI startup smoke.

title: Markdown file viewer/editor desktop app
description: Build a small Rust-based Windows desktop application that opens a positional Markdown file argument, shows a large resizable window, provides a minimal nav bar with a side-to-side toggle, switches between plaintext editing and formatted Markdown viewing, and preserves the current document location while toggling modes.
acceptance_criteria: Rust project builds; app accepts one positional .md path; default window is large and resizable but not fullscreen; nav bar is minimal and contains the view/edit toggle; plaintext mode supports editing and saving; formatted mode renders Markdown; toggling preserves the visible section/location; validation commands pass.
notes:
Originating user requirement is user-owned product direction from 2026-05-28.
MVP completed. Clarified title bar requirement is satisfied explicitly with decorated, resizable Wry window. File-association installer/registry setup remains out of scope for this first build; executable can be associated manually after build if desired.
close_reason: MVP implemented, reviewed, and validated locally.

title: Review GUI dependency choice
description: Compare lightweight Rust GUI approaches for this app and record the chosen approach with rationale and tradeoffs.
acceptance_criteria: Decision notes compare at least three viable approaches; chosen dependency set is justified for Windows Markdown viewer/editor use.
notes:
Decision review started. Comparing egui/eframe, Tauri, and Wry/WebView2 for a small Rust Windows Markdown reader/editor.
Dependency decision: compared (1) eframe/egui 0.34.3: pure Rust native GUI and no WebView, but rich Markdown rendering plus section-aware scroll sync would require more custom layout/control; (2) Tauri 2.11.2: mature desktop app framework using Wry, but adds project/build overhead and usually a frontend toolchain for a very small utility; (3) Wry 0.55.1 + WebView2 directly: Rust desktop shell with no Node build, built on the same webview layer Tauri uses, and the DOM makes textarea/rendered Markdown scroll mapping straightforward. Chosen approach: Wry direct shell with pulldown-cmark 0.13.x for Markdown conversion and ammonia 4.x for HTML sanitization. Tradeoff: Windows WebView2 runtime is expected; if absent, user may need the runtime installed.
close_reason: Chose direct Wry/WebView2 Rust shell after comparing egui/eframe, Tauri, and Wry. Decision recorded in Beads; implementation task will validate the dependency path.
